text: The overall injury incidence proportion among runners is approximately 40.2% and prevalence proportion is approximately 44.6%.
Achilles tendinopathy has the highest injury incidence proportion among specific running pathologies at 10.3%.

The five most common running injuries by incidence are Achilles tendinopathy (10.3%), medial tibial stress syndrome (9.4%), patellofemoral pain syndrome (6.3%), plantar fasciitis (6.1%), and ankle sprains (5.8%).

Patellofemoral pain syndrome has the highest injury prevalence proportion at 16.7%, followed by medial tibial stress syndrome (9.1%), plantar fasciitis (7.9%), iliotibial band syndrome (7.9%), and Achilles tendinopathy (6.6%).
The knee, ankle, and lower leg are the anatomic locations with the highest proportion of injury incidence in runners.
Among ultramarathon runners, the ankle (34.5%), knee (28.1%), and lower leg (12.9%) are the three most frequently injured anatomic sites.
Injury incidence proportions by anatomic location do not significantly differ between ultramarathon and non-ultramarathon runners.
